jdbc_connector_workflow.overview:
This workflow guides you through creating a custom JDBC connector for any database using the IBM Watson Data Platform Connect SDK. You can choose between two approaches:
1. Codeless Mode: Quick setup using a pre-built Docker image (gloria2000/wdp-connect-sdk-gen-flight:latest) - Best for standard JDBC connector use cases - No code generation or build process required - Faster deployment
2. Developer Mode: Full customizable connector code generation - Complete control over connector implementation - Includes code generation, customization, testing, and building - Best for custom implementations
Both modes end with deployment to AWS ECS Fargate.

prerequisites.requirement: Access to the target database for testing
JDBC driver information for the target database

phase.name: deployment_approach_selection
phase.title: Choose Deployment Approach
phase.description: Select between Codeless mode (using pre-built Docker image) or Developer mode (generating customizable connector code). This choice determines the workflow path.

step.action: Ask user to choose deployment approach
step.question: Choose your deployment approach for the JDBC connector:

suggestions.suggest: Codeless - Use pre-built Docker image (quick setup, standard use cases)
Developer - Generate customizable connector code (custom implementations)

step.action: Process user selection and set workflow path

actions.action: Inform user about pre-built Docker image usage
Create directory structure for DSL file
Proceed directly to Phase 10 (Flight App) with pre-built image
Inform user about custom code generation
Follow complete workflow through all phases

codeless_mode_details.overview: Codeless mode uses the pre-built Docker image: gloria2000/wdp-connect-sdk-gen-flight:latest

benefits.benefit: Best for standard JDBC connector use cases

directory_structure.purpose: Store DSL configuration file for the connector

workflow_impact.skip: Phase 2: DSL Creation (partial - only create DSL, no code gen)
workflow_impact.execute: Phase 10: Flight App Generation (using pre-built image)

developer_mode_details.overview: Developer mode generates full customizable connector code from scratch

phase.name: information_gathering
phase.title: Gather Database Information
phase.description: Collect all necessary information about the target database before starting the connector creation process.

required_information.item: Database name (e.g., db2, mysql, postgresql, oracle)
Default port number for the database
Whether database supports schemas, catalogs, or both

actions.action: Ask user for the database name they want to create a connector for
Determine if user has JDBC driver information or needs help finding it
Validate that we have all required information before proceeding

phase.name: dsl_creation
phase.title: Create DSL Configuration
phase.description: Create a database-specific DSL configuration file based on DSL-jdbc.json template. This file defines all connector properties, driver information, and customizations.

step.action: Create new DSL file named DSL-jdbc-{database}.json

fields.field: connector_description: Brief description of the connector

validation.check: Verify connector_label is user-friendly and descriptive
Ensure driver_class_name is correct for the database
Confirm jdbc_url template uses correct syntax
Validate driver_dependencies array is properly formatted

phase.name: label_confirmation
phase.title: Confirm Connector Label
phase.description: Ask the user to confirm the connector_label before proceeding, as this will be visible in the UI and should be user-friendly.

actions.action: Extract connector_label from the created DSL file
Use ask_followup_question to confirm with user
If user wants to change it, update the DSL file

phase.example_question: I've set the connector label to "{label}". This will be displayed in the Cloud Pak for Data UI. Is this label acceptable, or would you like to change it?

phase.name: code_generation
phase.title: Generate Connector Template Code
phase.description: Run the autoGenerateJavaJdbc Gradle task to generate the connector skeleton code from the DSL configuration.

step.action: Change to sdk-gen directory if not already there

step.note: The DSL file path is relative to sdk-gen directory

step.action: Wait for task completion and verify success

phase.name: dsl_relocation
phase.title: Move DSL to Connector Directory
phase.description: Move the DSL configuration file to the generated connector's resources directory for use in subsequent customization steps.

step.action: Create the target directory if it doesn't exist
Copy DSL file to connector directory
step.tool: execute_command with cp or use read_file + write_to_file

phase.name: connector_customization
phase.title: Customize Connector Code
phase.description: Run the customizeJdbcConnector task to apply DSL configuration to the generated connector code, updating driver class names, connection URLs, and properties.

what_it_does.update: Updates Connector.java with correct JDBC driver class name
Modifies getConnectionURL() method to build correct JDBC URL

phase.name: test_generation
phase.title: Generate Test Suite
phase.description: Generate comprehensive property validation tests for the connector using the generateJdbcTests task.

phase.name: code_formatting
phase.title: Apply Code Formatting
phase.description: Run spotlessApply to format all generated and customized code according to project standards.

phase.name: build
phase.title: Build Connector
phase.description: Build the connector without running tests to verify compilation and packaging.

phase.name: flight_app_generation
phase.title: Generate Flight App
phase.description: Automatically generate a Flight app project that includes the newly created connector. This creates a deployable application with the connector integrated.

step.note: This task automatically determines the connector name from the DSL file

success_indicators.indicator: Flight app directory created at subprojects/flight/
Connector dependency added to flight app build.gradle

step.action: Verify flight app can be built
step.note: This step is optional but recommended to verify the flight app is properly configured

what_it_does.action: Makes connector ready for Cloud Pak for Data integration

phase.name: aws_deployment
phase.title: AWS Deployment
phase.description: Deploy the connector to AWS ECS Fargate. This phase guides the user through configuring AWS deployment properties and running the deployment script.

step.action: Inform user about AWS deployment requirement
step.message: The connector will now be deployed to AWS ECS Fargate. You need to configure your AWS deployment settings in the aws-deployment.properties file.

step.action: Guide user to configure AWS properties
step.instructions: The deployment requires AWS configuration. You need to: 1. Copy aws-deployment.properties.template to aws-deployment.properties 2. Fill in your AWS details in aws-deployment.properties 3. Run the deployment script

step.message:
Please fill in the following required fields in aws-deployment.properties: - AWS_ACCOUNT_ID: Your 12-digit AWS account ID - AWS_REGION: AWS region (e.g., us-east-1) - VPC_ID: Your VPC ID (e.g., vpc-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx) - SUBNET_ID: Your subnet ID (e.g., subnet-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx) - ECR_IMAGE_URI: ECR image URI (format: account-id.dkr.ecr.region.amazonaws.com/repository-name:tag) - ECS_CLUSTER: ECS cluster name (will be created if it doesn't exist)
Optional: You can also configure AWS credentials in the file, or use AWS CLI configured credentials.

step.action: Wait for user confirmation that properties are configured
step.question: Have you filled in all the required AWS configuration details in aws-deployment.properties?

suggestions.suggest: Yes, configuration is complete and ready to deploy
No, I need more time to configure

step.note: The script will build the Docker image, push it to ECR, and deploy to ECS Fargate

what_it_does.action: Builds Docker image for linux/amd64 (AWS ECS Fargate compatible)
Creates ECR repository if it doesn't exist
Creates security group for the connector
Creates CloudWatch log group for logging
Creates IAM execution role for ECS tasks
Retrieves public IP address for the connector
Verifies deployment and displays connection details

prerequisites.requirement: Valid AWS credentials with appropriate permissions
Existing VPC and subnet in AWS

completion_criteria.criterion: DSL configuration file created and validated

criterion_conditional.criterion: DSL moved to connector resources directory
Directory structure created for DSL file

completion_criteria.criterion: Flight app generated with connector integrated

developer_mode.step: Review generated connector code in subprojects/jdbc_{database}/
Review generated flight app in subprojects/flight/

common_steps.step: Configure connector in Cloud Pak for Data using the gRPC endpoint

error.action: Check Gradle output for specific error messages
Verify DSL file syntax is valid JSON
Ensure all required fields are filled in DSL
Check that working directory is correct (sdk-gen/)
Consult database documentation for JDBC driver details
Check Maven Central for driver coordinates
Check for compilation errors in generated code
Verify JDBC driver dependency is correct
Check AWS CLI is installed and configured
Verify AWS credentials have appropriate permissions
Ensure VPC_ID and SUBNET_ID are valid and accessible
Review deployment script output for specific errors
